summary: Reactive [fleet] token refresh + single retry when the signed-command tesla-control subprocess fails with a 401, plus a skew-window-hardened proactive refresh.
reason: An agentcookie sink reading a synced config holds a short-lived [fleet] token that can lapse between sync and use (or read valid locally while Tesla rejects it). The owner-API read path self-heals via the transport OnTokenExpired hook, but the signed-command path shells out to tesla-control so the Go transport never sees that 401. Re-minting from the stored fleet refresh token and retrying once lets the sink control cars with no source-side fleet-refresh.
validated_outcome: Full module build/vet/test/govulncheck pass (310 cli tests). Covers: self-heal happy path (401 then retry succeeds, exactly 1 refresh + 2 subprocess calls), retry bounded to one, refresh-failure surfaces the original 401 with fleet-login guidance, non-auth failures (sleeping car/timeout) trigger no refresh, isFleetAuthError classification table, concurrent-refresh guard under -race, proactive skew-window table, and a read-path guard asserting both routing modes wire a 401 self-heal callback.
